response: The history of the internet began in the 1960s with US Department of Defense research, evolving from the 1969 ARPANET demonstration to the foundational adoption of TCP/IP protocols in 1983. Key milestones include Tim Berners-Lee’s invention of the World Wide Web in 1989 and the 2007 launch of the iPhone, which transitioned the web into a ubiquitous mobile experience. Despite the 2001 dot-com crash, the digital landscape expanded through the rise of social media, cloud computing, and massive tech giants. Today, the internet serves as a vital global infrastructure that connects over 5 billion people.
